zh.lifeSummary: 當Amy Amptyll於1548年出生在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特時，她的父親John Amptyll35歲，母親Amy Smith27歲。她於1564年11月5日與Thomas Empson在布利提申 韋斯特 英迪斯特立尼達及多巴哥維多利亞聖 瑪格麗特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1611年5月29日在她的家鄉享年63歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amy Amptyll于1548年出生在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特时，她的父亲John Amptyll35岁，母亲Amy Smith27岁。她于1564年11月5日与Thomas Empson在布利提申 韋斯特 英迪斯特立尼达和多巴哥維多利亞聖 瑪格麗特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1611年5月29日在她的家乡享年63岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可維斯特明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Amy Amptyll was born in 1548, in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Amptyll, was 35 and her mother, Amy Smith, was 27. She married Thomas Empson on 5 November 1564, in Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinidad and Tobago, British West Indies. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 29 May 1611, in her hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in Westminster, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Amy Amptyll est née en 1548, à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Amptyll, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Amy Smith, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Empson le 5 novembre 1564, à Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinité-et-Tobago, British West Indies. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 mai 1611, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Amy Amptyll 1548 in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Amptyll 35 und ihre Mutter Amy Smith 27. Sie hat am 5. November 1564 in Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinidad und Tobago, British West Indies Thomas Empson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. Mai 1611 im Alter von 63 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Westminster, Middlesex, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Amy Amptyllが1548年にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターで生まれた時，父John Amptyllは35歳，母Amy Smithは27歳であった。1564年11月5日にブリティッシュ ウエスト インディストリニダードトバゴヴィクトリアサイント マーガレットでThomas Empsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1611年5月29日に故郷で63歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Margaret's Church, Westminster, Lo: 브리티시 웨스트 인디스 트리니다드 토바고 빅토리아 세인트 마거릿

ko.lifeSummary: Amy Amptyll(이)가 1548년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Amptyll(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Amy Smith(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1564년 11월 5일 브리티시 웨스트 인디스 트리니다드 토바고 빅토리아 세인트 마거릿에서 Thomas Empson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1611년 5월 29일 그녀의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Amy Amptyll nasceu em 1548, em Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Amptyll, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Amy Smith, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Empson em 5 de novembro de 1564, em Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinidade e Tobago, British West Indies. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de maio de 1611, em sua cidade natal, com 63 anos, e foi sepultada em Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.St Giles in the Fields, Middlesex, Engla: ст джилесь ин т фиелдс, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amy Amptyll родилась в 1548 г. в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Amptyll было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Amy Smith, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Empson 5 ноября 1564 г. в местности сеинт маргарет, викторя, Тринидад и Тобаго, бритиш вест индиесь. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 29 мая 1611 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Amy Amptyll nació en 1548, en Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Amptyll, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Amy Smith, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Thomas Empson el 5 de noviembre de 1564, en Saint Margaret, Victoria, Trinidad y Tobago, British West Indies. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 29 de mayo de 1611, en su pueblo, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
